basicDetails.schemeName: Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme

schemeContent.detailedDescription_md: Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme (IGNWPS) is implemented by Ministry of Rural Development, Government of India. It is a non-contributory pension scheme to provide social security to widows of poor family (BPL)  of the society.
schemeContent.benefits_md: A pension of ₹ 300/- per month is provided to Widows between 40 years and 79 years. For persons who are 80 years and above, the pension is ₹ 500/- per month.
schemeContent.exclusions_md:
Pension will be discontinued
- In case of remarriage of widow - Once the widow moves above poverty line
schemeContent.briefDescription: A non-contributory pension scheme to provide social security to widows of poor family (BPL)  of the society.

children.text: Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme (IGNWPS) is implemented by Ministry of Rural Development, Government of India. It is a non-contributory pension scheme to provide social security to widows of poor family (BPL)  of the society.
A pension of ₹ 300/- per month is provided to Widows between 40 years and 79 years.  For persons who are 80 years and above, the pension is ₹ 500/- per month.
Pension will be discontinued
In case of remarriage of widow
Once the widow moves above poverty line
One can download UMANG App or visit website
https://web.umang.gov.in/web_new/home
The citizen can login using mobile number and OTP.
Once logged In, citizen can search for NSAP.
Click on “Apply Online”
Fill the basic details, choose the mode of payment of pension, upload photo and click on “Submit”.

applicationProcess.process_md: - One can download UMANG App or visit website [https://web.umang.gov.in/web_new/home](https://web.umang.gov.in/web_new/home) - The citizen can login using mobile number and OTP. - Once logged In, citizen can search for NSAP. - Click on “Apply Online” - Fill the basic details, choose the mode of payment of pension, upload photo and click on “Submit”.

children.text: Individuals can submit completely filled applications to the Gram Panchayat / Block Office in the rural area and the Municipality / Municipal Council in the urban area, as per eligibility.
A Verification Officer or Verification Team under an authorized officer verifies the applications with reference to facts related to eligibility
The Verification Officer make the necessary recommendation for sanction or rejection with reasons.

The list of applicants with the recommendations of the Verifying Authority is discussed in the Gram Sabha in rural areas or Ward Sabha / Area Sabha as designated by the State Government in urban areas and thereafter in the Gram Panchayats and Municipalities.

If the time limits are not adhered to by Gram Sabha / Ward Sabhas, Gram Panchayats / Municipalities the Verification Officer directly submits his recommendations to the Sanctioning Authority under intimation to the Gram Panchayat / Municipality.

After receipt of applications which are verified and recommended by the Gram Sabha / Ward Committee / Area Sabha, the Sanctioning Authority convey approval to the applicant in the form of a Sanction Order with a copy to Gram Panchayat / Municipality concerned.
The Sanctioning Authority issues Sanction Order under his seal

Every beneficiary who has been sanctioned pension under schemes of NSAP are issued a Pension Passbook. The Passbook contain details of the Sanction Order, particulars of the pensioner and disbursement details
The list of beneficiaries to whom sanctions are issued is displayed at the Gram Panchayat / Ward / Municipal Office and updated every three months.
The Pension amount is directly paid to the beneficiaries through the Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) process to their post office or bank account.

applicationProcess.process_md:
- Individuals can submit completely filled applications to the Gram Panchayat / Block Office in the rural area and the Municipality / Municipal Council in the urban area, as per eligibility.
- A Verification Officer or Verification Team under an authorized officer verifies the applications with reference to facts related to eligibility
- The Verification Officer make the necessary recommendation for sanction or rejection with reasons.
- The list of applicants with the recommendations of the Verifying Authority is discussed in the Gram Sabha in rural areas or Ward Sabha / Area Sabha as designated by the State Government in urban areas and thereafter in the Gram Panchayats and Municipalities.
- If the time limits are not adhered to by Gram Sabha / Ward Sabhas, Gram Panchayats / Municipalities the Verification Officer directly submits his recommendations to the Sanctioning Authority under intimation to the Gram Panchayat / Municipality.
- After receipt of applications which are verified and recommended by the Gram Sabha / Ward Committee / Area Sabha, the Sanctioning Authority convey approval to the applicant in the form of a Sanction Order with a copy to Gram Panchayat / Municipality concerned.
- The Sanctioning Authority issues Sanction Order under his seal
- Every beneficiary who has been sanctioned pension under schemes of NSAP are issued a Pension Passbook. The Passbook contain details of the Sanction Order, particulars of the pensioner and disbursement details
- The list of beneficiaries to whom sanctions are issued is displayed at the Gram Panchayat / Ward / Municipal Office and updated every three months. 
- The Pension amount is directly paid to the beneficiaries through the Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) process to their post office or bank account.

eligibilityCriteria.eligibilityDescription_md: The eligibility criteria under Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme (IGNWPS) is: - The applicant must be a widow in the age group of 40-79 years. - The applicant should belong to a household living below the poverty line according to the criteria prescribed by the Govt. of India

children.text: The eligibility criteria under Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme (IGNWPS) is:
The applicant must be a widow in the age group of 40-79 years.
The applicant should belong to a household living below the poverty line according to the criteria prescribed by the Govt. of India
Death Certificate of husband – With the name of the surviving wife(widow).
BPL Card.

Age Proof - For age, the birth certificate or school certificate may be relied on. In their absence ration card and EPIC may be considered. If there is no valid document, any Medical Officer of any government hospital may be authorized to issue the age certificate.

faqs.question: What are different modes of receiving Pension?

children.text: One can receive pension amount in Bank Account, Post Office Account, Postal Money Order or in hand cash.

faqs.answer_md: One can receive pension amount in Bank Account, Post Office Account, Postal Money Order or in hand cash.

faqs.question: Who will disburse in hand cash?

children.text: In hand cash will be disbursed by Pension Disbursement Authority of respective Sub District/Municipal Area. One can select the pension disbursement authority while filling online application form on UMANG.

faqs.answer_md: In hand cash will be disbursed by Pension Disbursement Authority of respective Sub District/Municipal Area. One can select the pension disbursement authority while filling online application form on UMANG.

faqs.question: What pension amount is provided under the scheme?

children.text: Eligible beneficiaries receive Central Assistance of ₹300 per month. Beneficiaries aged 80 years and above receive ₹500 per month.

faqs.answer_md: Eligible beneficiaries receive Central Assistance of ₹300 per month. Beneficiaries aged 80 years and above receive ₹500 per month.

faqs.question: Is there any income criterion for availing the scheme?

children.text: Yes. The applicant must belong to a household identified as living Below Poverty Line (BPL) as per the criteria prescribed by the Government of India.

faqs.answer_md: Yes. The applicant must belong to a household identified as living Below Poverty Line (BPL) as per the criteria prescribed by the Government of India.

faqs.question: Can a widow above 80 years continue receiving benefits?

children.text: Yes. Upon attaining 80 years of age, the beneficiary becomes eligible for enhanced assistance of ₹500 per month.

faqs.answer_md: Yes. Upon attaining 80 years of age, the beneficiary becomes eligible for enhanced assistance of ₹500 per month.

faqs.question: How can an eligible widow apply for the pension?

children.text: Applications can be submitted through the prescribed process followed by the concerned State Government, Gram Panchayat, Municipality, or designated welfare authority.

faqs.answer_md: Applications can be submitted through the prescribed process followed by the concerned State Government, Gram Panchayat, Municipality, or designated welfare authority.

faqs.question: How is the pension amount disbursed to beneficiaries?

children.text: The pension is generally transferred directly to the beneficiary's bank or post office account through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT).

faqs.answer_md: The pension is generally transferred directly to the beneficiary's bank or post office account through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT).

faqs.question: Can a widow receive benefits from both Central and State pension schemes?

children.text: States may provide additional assistance over and above the Central contribution, subject to their respective rules and policies.

faqs.answer_md: States may provide additional assistance over and above the Central contribution, subject to their respective rules and policies.

faqs.question: What is the objective of the Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme?

children.text: The scheme aims to provide financial security and social assistance to destitute widows from economically vulnerable households, helping them meet their basic needs and live with dignity.

faqs.answer_md: The scheme aims to provide financial security and social assistance to destitute widows from economically vulnerable households, helping them meet their basic needs and live with dignity.
